issue_title: Feature Request: Use debug middleware to show number of queries executed?
issue_body: To more easily solve n+1 and related problems, can we add the 'total number of queries executed by this graphql query' to the debug middleware? Is this even possible?

**Story:**

As a System Administrator I want the ability to navigate to Instance Settings > Get System log in the AWS Console in order to verify that Watchmaker ran successfully.

**Back of the card:**

System Administrators should not need to SSH or RDP into each Instance to verify that WAM ran. They should be able to verify based on the contents of the system log within the AWS console.

- If WAM ran successfully with no errors or warnings a "Watchmaker Status: Success!" message should appear in the system log

- If WAM encountered a warning or error a "Watchmaker Status: <error / warning>" should be in the system log. For more detailed messages or stack traces the watchmaker.log file works just fine

### Describe the problem

When you change one of the constance fields at the admin page it will dispatch the signal for every field, no matter if was changed or not.

It should only send the signal when an actual change was made on a field.

I took a look into https://github.com/jazzband/django-constance/blob/03704ee4f7cc70b914b1305d1e1f21b0138ddd77/constance/admin.py#L110 and the `save` method goes through all the fields without checking if it was changed.

I added a check, but I had a problem checking datetime objects, because when I get the object from the form is a datetime object and when I get the object from the `config` object it returns a string.

The comparison also affects the `modified` status since we are comparing a string and a datetime objects.

### Steps to reproduce

Use datetime objects at `CONSTANCE_CONFIG`:
```
    (
        'DISABLE_ORDERS_SINCE',
        (
            timezone(TIME_ZONE).localize(datetime(2015, 12, 24, 20)),
            'redacted',
            datetime,
        )
    ),
    (
        'DISABLE_ORDERS_UNTIL',
        (
            timezone(TIME_ZONE).localize(datetime(2016, 1, 2, 11)),
            'redacted',
            datetime,
        )
    ),
    (
        'STORE_OPENS_AT',
        ('12:00:00', 'redacted', time)
    ),
```

### System configuration

* Django version: 1.8, 1.9, 1.10
* Python version: 2.7, 3.5, pypy

### Possible solution

I did test:
```
--- a/constance/admin.py
+++ b/constance/admin.py
@@ -108,8 +108,7 @@ class ConstanceForm(forms.Form):

     def save(self):
         for name in settings.CONFIG:
-            if str(getattr(config, name)) != str(self.cleaned_data[name]):
-                setattr(config, name, self.cleaned_data[name])
+            setattr(config, name, self.cleaned_data[name])

     def clean_version(self):
         value = self.cleaned_data['version']
@@ -151,7 +150,7 @@ class ConstanceAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
             'default': localize(default),
             'help_text': _(help_text),
             'value': localize(value),
-            'modified': str(value) != str(default),
+            'modified': value != default,
             'form_field': form[name],
         }
```

But it only works on python 3.5

issue_title: Ruby gRPC client generation should be merged into gapic pipeline
issue_body:
The gcloud-ruby project actually bundles gRPC generated files as a part of the google-cloud libraries (for example, gcloud-ruby/google-cloud-pubsub contains the gRPC files at https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-ruby/tree/master/google-cloud-pubsub/lib/google/pubsub/v1).

For non-cloud APIs, I think either design (i.e. merging into a single package or separating Gapic client from gRPC client) would be okay. So because of this gcloud-ruby design, I think all Ruby's generated artifacts will follow this style.

The current design of Ruby's grpc client generation pipeline doesn't fit with this design. It should:
- [ ] stop using packman but use the protoc command directly
- [ ] introduce this protoc task as a part of Ruby's Gapic pipeline -- so that the generated files reside in a same package

issue_title: Remove block numbers from the smart contract events
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

[NettingChannel](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/b70cd3d5074ed4605b3a643834b74dc5c5604ed7/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelContract.sol#L9-L12) expose a few events with the block number, that value is duplicated since it's available through the [eth_getFilterChanges](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_getfilterchanges)

> blockNumber: QUANTITY - the block number where this log was in. null when its pending. null when its pending log.

## Solution

Remove the `block_number` fields from all smart contract event and use the result from the JSON RPC call.

## Tasklist

- [ ] remove the block_number
  - [ ] ChannelNewBalance
  - [ ] ChannelClosed
  - [ ] TransferUpdated
  - [ ] ChannelSettled
- [ ] Expose the `blockNumber` from the log to the [filter changes](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/b70cd3d5074ed4605b3a643834b74dc5c5604ed7/raiden/network/rpc/client.py#L332-L357)
- [ ] Fix the code that needs the block number.

issue_title: Handle old balance proofs used for settlement by the channel partner
issue_body:
Raiden client keeps the last balance proof data in the channel state.
If the partner uses an older balance proof, there is currently no implemented way to find that data.

As @hackaugusto pointed out, we can either iterate through all the WAL data (bytes encoded) and decode it and check if it is what we are looking for, or change the current storage layout and add an additional table.

issue_title: Use `require` and `assert` in the smart contracts
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Since version 0.4.10 solidity has introduced the `assert()` and `require()` [commands](https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/develop/control-structures.html?highlight=assert). Our smart contracts don't use them yet and it would be beneficial to start using them for both readability but also for assisting with future formal verification tools.

## Solution

As stated in the [documentation](https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/develop/control-structures.html?highlight=assert):

>If contracts are written so that `assert` is only used to test internal conditions and `require` is used in case of malformed input, a formal analysis tool that verifies that the invalid opcode can never be reached can be used to check for the absence of errors assuming valid inputs.

we should try to replace all `if (bad_condition) { throw; }` statements with `assert` and `revert` where appropriate.

## Tasklist

- [ ] All checks that have to do with invalid user input (such as [this](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/a62adaaf374f0188990dfe324bcc9f73ef75cd37/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol#L221)) or invalid user action (such as [this](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/a62adaaf374f0188990dfe324bcc9f73ef75cd37/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol#L133)) should be replaced with `require`
- [ ] If there is any check that is there to protect against programming error and generally things that should never happen but **do not** depend on user input then it should be replaced with an `assert`
- [ ] Replace the solidity pragma with `pragma solidity ^0.4.10` to indicate we now need newer solidity to use the `require` and `assert`;

When a job is executed on the remote server, the results would be available on the remote host, with status `result-ready`. Generally speaking, if we are running in `--no-wait` mode, we will need to run `sos run -q` at least twice, the first time get the tasks submitted, and the second time to collect the results (if the tasks are ready), and continue to workflow if needed.

```
sos run script -q remote
sos run script -q remote
```

For a particular task, we can run

```
sos execute task_id -q remote
```

and if the task has already been done, this command would simply sync the result to the local host. Then, it might make sense to add an option to this command

```
sos execute task_id -q remote --fetch-result
```

so that it does not do anything if the result is not ready (namely do not actually execute the task). We might be able to expand the syntax to something like

```
sos execute -q remote --fetch-result --all
```
to check and retrieve results for all remote tasks.

That said, a dedicated `fetch` command might be more intuitive.

```
sos fetch task_id -q remote
sos fetch --all -q remote
```

issue_title: How to set PROXIES for requests?
issue_body:
I was looking for a way to set proxy in order to make requests.
At the moment I've patched base backend:
`
proxies = getattr(settings, 'SOCIAL_SERVICES_PROXIES', {})
old_request = BaseAuth.request

@wraps(old_request)
def new_request(self, url, method='GET', *args, **kwargs):
    '''
    COPY-PASTE from social_core.backends.base import BaseAuth
    with proxy support
    '''
    kwargs.setdefault('headers', {})
    if self.setting('VERIFY_SSL') is not None:
        kwargs.setdefault('verify', self.setting('VERIFY_SSL'))
    kwargs.setdefault('timeout', self.setting('REQUESTS_TIMEOUT') or self.setting('URLOPEN_TIMEOUT'))
    if self.SEND_USER_AGENT and 'User-Agent' not in kwargs['headers']:
        kwargs['headers']['User-Agent'] = self.setting('USER_AGENT') or user_agent()

    kwargs.setdefault('proxies', proxies)  # main line

    try:
        if self.SSL_PROTOCOL:
            session = SSLHttpAdapter.ssl_adapter_session(self.SSL_PROTOCOL)
            response = session.request(method, url, *args, **kwargs)
        else:
            response = request(method, url, *args, **kwargs)
    except ConnectionError as err:
        LOG.info(str(kwargs))
        LOG.error(str(err))
        raise AuthFailed(self, str(err))
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response

BaseAuth.request = new_request
`
Is there a better way to do that?

This is followup for #16. 

With #25 the migration from 0.2.20 and 0.2.21 is working fine, however users upgrading from 0.2.19 and older will still end up with error.

It can end up with errorr described in #16:

```
KeyError: u"Migration social_django.0002_add_related_name dependencies reference nonexistent parent node (u'social_django', u'0001_initial')"
```

Or it can pop up as error while validating relations (see https://github.com/WeblateOrg/weblate/issues/1344):

```
ValueError: The field accounts.VerifiedEmail.social was declared with a lazy reference to 'social_django.usersocialauth', but app 'social_django' isn't installed.
```

The problem is that Django does not see the old migrations as they are just half applied (due to missing `social_auth` ones:

```
manage.py showmigrations
...
social_django
 [ ] 0005_auto_20160727_2333 (1 squashed migrations)
 [ ] 0006_partial
...
```

issue_title: Ops doc leaks info about the attr of kind "list of ints"
issue_body:
Hi, 
After lot of time doing retro-engineering on ONNX model, it seems that the attr of kind "list of ints" like strides or kernel_shape of max pool por example are only for Height and Width axes and not for all input axes.
But in the doc we can read : 

> strides : list of ints
>    Stride along each axis. 

So it is very unclear.

Moreover, this king of op doesn't permit to do MaxPool with kernel_shape different of 1 on the batch dimension and different from 1 for channel dimension.

So it would be good to add Ops that can make these tasks. 
Or to modify the attr of these Ops in order to have attr of same len than input dim.
For example, if input is NCHW the attr kernel_shape should be [1, 3, 2, 2] or [16, 3, 2, 2] if we want to apply max_pool over all channels of over all batch input and all channel.

It would be more generic and would cost nothing to add that in ONNX.

issue_title: Gemm requires (MxK), (KxN) inputs but no reshape are inserted in onnx models.
issue_body:
I facing a problem when working with "bvlc_alexnet/model.pb" from [here](https://github.com/onnx/models/tree/master/bvlc_alexnet)

Its include a *Gemm* node with 3 inputs:
- The output of the previous node : pool_5, its shape is : [1, 256, 6, 6]
- A weights tensor presents into the initializers. its shape is : [4096, 9216]
- A bias tensor presents into initializers. its shape is : [4096]

The attrs of the node are : 
- Broadcasting = 1 : it's ok to broadcast the bias tensor.
- transB = 1 : it's ok to transpose the weights tensor.

In the [doc](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#gemm) we can read : 

> where input tensor A has dimension (M X K) , input tensor B has dimension (K X N), input tensor C and output tensor Y have dimension (M X N)

So the tensor B will have shape : [9216, 4096] after being transposed.
Si the input A (the output of the previous node) MUST have the shape : [1, 256*6*6] = [1, 9216]
This will produce a tensor of shape [1, 4096] which we can add to the bias tensor (input C).

According to me, a reshape is missing between the pool_5 node and the Gemm node.

issue_title: Does not use real SPARQL for RDFa test suite
issue_body:
vfaronov, 2010-09-20T12:59:02.000Z 
run_w3c_rdfa_testsuite.py
does not use a real SPARQL engine for answering the ASK queries as part of the RDFa test suite. Instead, it always uses an ad-hoc routine for extracting graph patterns. Apparently it wasn't adjusted when SPARQL support was moved to rdfextras. The following patch adds SPARQL(rdfextras) support back to this test suite, which makes TC #105 and #106 pass, hence they are removed from KNOWN_ISSUES. It leaves the ability to fall back to "_sparql_to_graphs" if real SPARQL doesn't work. ```diff Index: test/rdfa/run_w3c_rdfa_testsuite.py =================================================================== --- test/rdfa/run_w3c_rdfa_testsuite.py (revision 1896) +++ test/rdfa/run_w3c_rdfa_testsuite.py (working copy) @@ -11,6 +11,11 @@ It is intended to be run by Nose (but can also be invoked as a script). +By default, the runner will try to connect the SPARQL implementation from the +`rdfextras` package for answering the tests' ASK queries. If that package is +not available, or if the SPARQL engine fails for some reason, the runner will +resort to an ad-hoc mechanism for extracting graph patterns from the queries. + If files in "test/rdfa/w3c_rdfa_suite" are removed, the test module automatically downloads them (to allow for "manual+automatic" update). """ @@ -20,6 +25,7 @@ from urllib2 import urlopen from urllib import url2pathname +from rdflib import plugin, query from rdflib.graph import Graph from rdflib.namespace import Namespace, RDF from rdflib.parser import create_input_source @@ -33,6 +39,19 @@ "testsuite/xhtml1-testcases/rdfa-xhtml1-test-manifest.rdf") +# Try to connect the "real" SPARQL implementation from rdfextras. +try: + plugin.register( + 'sparql', query.Processor, + 'rdfextras.sparql.processor', 'Processor') + plugin.register( + 'sparql', query.Result, + 'rdfextras.sparql.query', 'SPARQLQueryResult') +except: + print "WARNING: using ad-hoc answering instead of SPARQL for ASKs" + print + + class TestCase(object): def __init__(self, graph, tc_uri): val = lambda p: graph.value(tc_uri, p) @@ -113,7 +132,6 @@ KNOWN_ISSUES = set([11, 92, 94, 100, 101, 102, 103, 114, 117]) -KNOWN_ISSUES |= set([105, 106]) def all_tests(skip_known_issues=True): ```
